Metadata.seoTitle: X-Glass | Fujifilm X-Mount Lens Database & Comparison
Metadata.seoDescription: Every Fujifilm X-Mount lens, normalized and ready to compare — XF, XC, MK, Sigma, Tamron, Viltrox, TTArtisan, 7Artisans, Brightin Star, SG Image. One database, zero bias.

About.backgroundBody1: The Fujifilm X-mount lens ecosystem has grown from a handful of native options in its early days to today's landscape of dozens of brands and hundreds of lenses. Yet specs are scattered across brand websites and various platforms in inconsistent formats, making meaningful comparison a slow and frustrating exercise. X-Glass was built to pull all of that fragmented information into one place — a normalized, comparable database that lets the data speak for itself.
About.backgroundBody2: X-Glass was created by a former software engineer at a major tech company. A deep conviction about engineering quality and user experience has always been a driving force — and that same conviction is part of what made this project possible.
About.coverageBody: X-Glass aims to cover every X-mount lens ever made, including discontinued models. Due to the scale of the data pipeline, the current index starts from lenses listed on each brand's official website, so some discontinued lenses no longer featured there are temporarily missing. Eight major brands are covered in this first release; expanding to more active brands is on the roadmap for a future phase. Adapted lenses from other mounts are outside the current scope.
About.coverageBrandList: Fujifilm · Sigma · Tamron · Viltrox · TTArtisan · 7Artisans · Brightin Star · SG Image
About.dataAccuracyIntro: For a database built on objective information, accuracy isn't a bonus — it's the foundation. If users can't trust the data, the site has no reason to exist.
About.dataPrinciple1Title: All data is sourced from official manufacturer information
About.dataPrinciple1Body: Nothing in this database was autonomously collected or generated by AI. AI Agents assist at the collection and structured parsing stages, but every step involves human review. The pipeline is built with strict stage isolation: each step can only draw from facts confirmed in the previous step, and the model is never asked to infer or guess.
About.dataPrinciple2Title: If it can be computed deterministically, natural language doesn't touch it
About.dataPrinciple2Body: LLMs can process anything expressed in natural language — which is both a strength and a risk. For a database that demands precision, even a very small error rate is unacceptable. Fields that can be derived from rules or formulas are computed in code. Only the parts that require genuine semantic understanding are passed to the model.
About.dataPipelineIntro: X-Glass lens data is produced through a multi-stage pipeline:
About.pipeline0Desc: Scripts index lens listings from each brand's official website
About.pipeline1Desc: AI Agents collect official lens descriptions and hero images from manufacturer sources
About.pipeline2Desc: Script rules compute deterministic fields; AI Agents derive semantic fields
About.pipelineRDesc: Maintainer reviews each entry and applies any manual overrides
About.pipelinePDesc: Validation, normalization, and final merge into the database
About.dataUpdateNote: The database is updated when new lenses launch or when existing data needs correction.
About.dataVersionNote: The version number and last-updated timestamp in the footer reflect the latest published data release.
About.faq1Q: A lens I'm looking for isn't here. Why?
About.faq1A: We're working toward complete X-mount coverage, but the database grows incrementally and some lenses may still be missing. Use the feedback links at the bottom of this page to suggest one. That's the fastest way to put it on our radar.
About.faq2Q: A spec doesn't match the manufacturer's page. Which is correct?
About.faq2A: Always treat the manufacturer's official page as the source of truth. Our data can contain mistakes or lag behind spec revisions. If you spot a mismatch, use the "Report a data issue" link on the lens detail page.
About.faq3Q: Is X-Glass affiliated with Fujifilm or any lens brand?
About.faq3A: No. X-Glass is an independent project with no affiliation to Fujifilm or any other manufacturer. Lens names and brand marks are the property of their respective owners.
About.faq4A: A dash means the data hasn't been collected yet, or the manufacturer hasn't publicly published that spec.
About.disclaimerSources: Specifications are sourced primarily from manufacturer official websites and product pages.
About.disclaimerAccuracy: Data can contain errors, and specs may change without notice. Always verify important details directly with the manufacturer before making a purchase decision.
About.disclaimerLiability: X-Glass is not responsible for any decisions made based on information presented here.
About.privacyBody: X-Glass does not require user accounts and does not collect personal information.
About.donationBody: X-Glass is free and will stay free. If you find it useful, you're welcome to show your support through any of the options below.
About.ackBody: I could not have imagined building something at this scale on my own, let alone in under a month. It could take forever for me without large language models or AI coding agents. I am genuinely grateful for the era of technology we live in — one where ideas that once felt impossibly ambitious can actually be brought to life.
About.feedbackBody: Spotted incorrect data or a missing lens? Your report goes straight to the maintainer.

Feedback.descriptionDataIssue: Wrong spec, broken image, typo, weird detail — tell us what you found. It goes straight to the maintainer.
Feedback.descriptionMissingLens: Know an X-mount lens we're missing? Send it over and we'll add it to the queue.
Feedback.descriptionGeneral: Thoughts, bug reports, and feature ideas are all welcome.
Feedback.contentRequired: Add either a suggested correction or a description.
Feedback.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the issue with as much detail as you'd like…
Feedback.success: Thanks, your feedback is in. We'll take a look soon.
Feedback.error: Something went wrong. Please try again in a bit.

Install.headline: Add X-Glass to your home screen
Install.subheadline: Faster access, full-screen experience, works offline.
Install.installedBody: X-Glass is already installed on this device.
Install.promptBody: Add it to your home screen or app launcher for one-tap access.
Install.iosStep1: Tap the Share button (the square with an arrow) at the bottom of Safari
Install.iosStep2: Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen"
Install.iosNotSafari: For the full standalone experience, open this page in Safari. You can still add it from your current browser.
Install.genericBody: Open your browser menu and look for "Add to Home Screen" or "Install app".
